title: abrus aureus subsp. littoralis
intro: Abrus aureus subsp. littoralis, also known as the golden chain tree, is a tropical legume native to the coastal regions of Africa and Asia, characterized by its bright yellow and orange flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which have been shown to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, pain, and inflammation, as well as treating wounds and skin conditions. The medicinal properties of Abrus aureus subsp. littoralis can be utilized through various herbal preparations, such as infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which can be applied topically or ingested to reap its benefits. Furthermore, the plant is rich in flavonoids, alkaloids, and other bioactive compounds that contribute to its medicinal and therapeutic effects.
